title: Add Pod and Container namespaces
body:
The latest monster of a PR I've worked up. Sorry to reviewers for the size...

This adds the concept of namespaces to libpod. A namespace is a group of containers and pods that can (optionally) be managed separately from the rest of the state. When namespace is set, only containers and pods in the same namespace will be visible, and all new containers and pods will automatically join the namespace. If namespace is not set (the empty string, internally), we can see containers and pods from all namespaces, including those with no namespace.

This is a step towards CRI-O integration, as CRI-O will not want to see any containers and pods except the ones it created itself.

comments.body:
am I doing something wrong?

with a fresh storage:
```console
# podman --namespace foo run --name ctr1 alpine true
# podman --namespace bar run --name ctr1 alpine true
# podman --namespace foo ps -a
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE                             COMMAND   CREATED          STATUS                              PORTS   NAMES
703728ee8268   docker.io/library/alpine:latest   true      12 seconds ago   Exited (0) Less than a second ago           ctr2
57b3006154d3   docker.io/library/alpine:latest   true      31 seconds ago   Exited (0) Less than a second ago           ctr1
# podman --namespace bar ps -a
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE                             COMMAND   CREATED          STATUS                              PORTS   NAMES
703728ee8268   docker.io/library/alpine:latest   true      17 seconds ago   Exited (0) Less than a second ago           ctr2
57b3006154d3   docker.io/library/alpine:latest   true      36 seconds ago   Exited (0) Less than a second ago           ctr1
```

shouldn't only one container be visible for both `ps` commands?
@giuseppe This is what happens when I don't write integration tests. Oops. Fixing...
Not sure what's going on with the tests, they're passing locally.
Nevermind, silly error on my part. Should be good once tests run
Comments addressed, should be good for merge.
@mtrmac Take one more pass and we can merge.
Papr still seems to be hosed.  I am relying on Travis and will merge manually.
